name: Intercredit Bank, National Association
description_short: FDIC-insured national bank headquartered in Miami offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and international banking services with bilingual support.
description_long:
Intercredit Bank, National Association is a federally-chartered bank based in Miami, Florida that has been serving consumers and businesses with personalized banking relationships. The bank emphasizes a relationship-focused approach with the tagline "At Intercredit, business is always personal," positioning itself as an alternative to larger national banks by offering personalized attention rather than automated, impersonal service.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of products for both personal and business customers. Personal banking includes checking and savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, debit cards, residential loans, credit cards, and digital services like online banking, mobile deposit, bill pay, and peer-to-peer transfers. Business customers can access commercial checking and savings accounts, commercial real estate loans, construction financing, SBA 504 real estate loans, term loans, corporate structured financing, and business credit cards. Additionally, Intercredit serves international customers with tailored accounts, loans, and payment services, including foreign exchange wire transfers and letters of credit.

Intercredit differentiates itself through several specific features: it operates physical branches with Monday-Friday lobby hours while offering 24/7 mobile banking, provides access to Presto! Network ATMs at no extra charge, explicitly supports small business growth, and offers specialized services for international customers. The bank also maintains bilingual support in English and Spanish and is transparent about its FDIC insurance coverage and regulatory compliance (Member FDIC, Equal Housing Lender).

As a regional bank based in Miami, Intercredit serves a specific geographic and customer base, particularly those seeking relationship-driven banking and businesses needing specialized commercial real estate and international financing. However, the website provides limited detail on specific product terms, rates, and fees beyond indicating a Schedule of Fees is available. Customers should expect to contact the bank directly or visit branches for detailed pricing information on loans, credit cards, and deposit products.
services: Savings accounts and CDs with IRA options
Residential and commercial real estate loans
Commercial term loans and construction financing
Online banking and mobile app banking
Bill pay, wire transfers, and peer-to-peer transfers
Letters of credit for business customers
pros: FDIC-insured with full U.S. government backing on deposits
No-fee access to Presto! Network ATMs nationwide
Bilingual banking support in English and Spanish
24/7 mobile app banking with mobile deposit capability
Specialized commercial real estate lending for business customers
International banking services including foreign exchange wires and letters of credit
Physical branch locations open Monday-Friday for in-person service
cons: Limited geographic footprint as a regional bank (Miami-based with specific branch locations)
Website lacks transparent rates, fees, and product terms—requires contacting bank directly
No mention of competitive interest rates on savings accounts or CDs compared to online banks
International services appear limited to premium/business banking tiers
Mobile app requires separate credit card access login, suggesting less integrated digital experience
best_for: Small business owners seeking personalized commercial real estate financing
International customers and businesses needing foreign exchange and cross-border payment services
Miami-based individuals and businesses preferring relationship-driven banking with bilingual support
Customers wanting 24/7 mobile banking combined with accessible physical branch locations
diagnosis: Intercredit Bank is best for small business owners, international customers, and Miami-area residents seeking relationship-focused banking with personalized service rather than automated alternatives. The main caveat is that as a regional bank, it has limited physical presence outside Miami and offers fewer online-only conveniences than national neobanks; customers need direct contact with the bank to obtain specific rates and fees.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
